---
title: 'Amazing Grace | Amazing Husband | Church Notes 11/08/14'
date: '2014-11-08T22:00:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '897362051920330745'
blogger_filename: '/2014/11/amazing-grace-amazing-husband-church.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Spiritual Growth'
---

<div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"></div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr"><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/amazing-grace-amazing-husband-6bd3607c74b7.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt=" Gateway Church | Southlake Texas | Saturday 11/08/14      Series: Amazing Grace     Message: Amazing Husband" border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/amazing-grace-amazing-husband-6bd3607c74b7.png" height="251" title=" Gateway Church | Southlake Texas | Saturday 11/08/14      Series: Amazing Grace     Message: Amazing Husband" width="320" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;"><a href="http://gatewaypeople.com/sermons/402181" target="_blank">Pastor Robert Morris Gateway Church 11/08/14</a></td></tr></tbody></table><br /></div><h3 dir="ltr">Gateway Church | Southlake Texas | Saturday 11/08/14</h3><ul><li><h4>Series: Amazing Grace</h4></li><li><h4>Message: <a href="http://gatewaypeople.com/sermons/402181" target="_blank">Amazing Husband</a> </h4></li></ul><h4 dir="ltr">These are my notes from service this weekend. They will not be a point by point synopsis; rather, they will be the things that stuck out to me, as they occurred to me, in no particular order. </h4><div dir="ltr"><h4>Notes:</h4><br /></div><div dir="ltr">Law gives strength to sin. When we focus on the rules and <a href="http://dictionary.reference.com/browse/do%27s+and+don%27ts" target="_blank">do's and don'ts</a>, we actually give power to the force of sin and temptation. Grace shows us that Jesus died for sin... all sin. All the sin I every have done, am doing, will do, is paid for in the blood of Jesus. He is not judging me based on my sin. He is seeking a relationship. He is showing me that I can come to him as I am, without trying to change. That doesn't just apply to the sinner getting saved, that applies to the religious Christian, who's been serving God for decades, and "trying so hard" to be a good Christian. There is no point at which my actions or behaviors or habits, or sins are within my ability to change. I can and must take certain actions toward change, but the action I take to change is not to alter my behavior, it is to surrender that behavior to God. I come to Him, and ask Him to do in me, what I cannot do for myself. The first three steps, of the famous <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twelve-step_program#Twelve_Steps" target="_blank">12 step program "AA"</a> are based on this key of Grace:</div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><ol><li>We admitted we were powerless over "xxxx(fill in the sin)"â€”and that our lives had become unmanageable.</li><li>Came to believe that a power greater than ourselves could restore us to sanity.&nbsp;</li><ol><li>(The blood of Jesus)</li></ol><li>Made a decision to turn our will and our lives over to the care of God <i>as we understood Him</i>.&nbsp;</li><ol><li>(I will understand God better today than I did 10 years ago, and better tomorrow than today. It's merely important that I start, not that I start perfectly or with perfect knowledge.)</li></ol></ol><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr"><a href="http://bible.com/97/1co.15.51-57.msg" target="_blank">1 Corinthians 15</a> Message Version</div></blockquote><blockquote>But let me tell you something wonderful, a mystery I'll probably never fully understand. We're not all going to dieâ€” but we are all going to be changed. You hear a blast to end all blasts from a trumpet, and in the time that you look up and blink your eyesâ€”it's over. On signal from that trumpet from heaven, the dead will be up and out of their graves, beyond the reach of death, never to die again. At the same moment and in the same way, we'll all be changed. In the resurrection scheme of things, this has to happen: everything perishable taken off the shelves and replaced by the imperishable, this mortal replaced by the immortal. Then the saying will come true: Death swallowed by triumphant Life! Who got the last word, oh, Death? Oh, Death, who's afraid of you now? It was sin that made death so frightening and law-code guilt that gave sin its leverage, its destructive power. But now in a single victorious stroke of Life, all threeâ€”sin, guilt, deathâ€”are gone, the gift of our Master, Jesus Christ. Thank God! </blockquote><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr">As love grows cold more law is necessary, grace has  more power to govern than law. Where grace is absent, tolerance and anti  discrimination laws increase. As we are motivated by love, we automatically seek to do right by others. As more people reject God and Jesus (Love Himself) they are rejecting love and replacing it with tolerance. Tolerance is the evil twisted version of Love without God. Tolerance is by nature, intolerant of anything and everything. There are laws popping up all over the land that are actually hindering people's free speech and ability to discuss ideas openly and honestly, all in the name of tolerance. That is what happens when a society abandons Love Himself... they become law conscious. </div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div dir="ltr"><a href="http://bible.com/107/1co.15.56.net" target="_blank">1 Corinthians 15:56 NET Version </a></div></blockquote><blockquote>The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.</blockquote><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr">Law is your old spouse. Try serving your new spouse by serving your old spouse. Doesn't work. Your wife would not be happy if you spent all your time trying to make you old wife happy. Stop trying to serve law, rules, do's, don'ts, etc. Instead, surrender to Love and Grace.&nbsp;</div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div dir="ltr"><a href="http://bible.com/97/gal.3.1.msg" target="_blank">Galatians 3:1 Message Version </a></div>You crazy Galatians! Did someone put a hex on you? Have you taken leave  of your senses? Something crazy has happened, for it's obvious that you  no longer have the crucified Jesus in clear focus in your lives. His  sacrifice on the cross was certainly set before you clearly enough.</blockquote><div dir="ltr">&nbsp; </div><div dir="ltr">&nbsp;Thinking of right and wrong is law, life is relationship. If I said to my wife: "Honey, I will never cheat on you, even though I really want to, because the law says not to..." would that bring comfort to her? NO! You'd be divorced soon after, probably. But if you said: "Honey, I will never cheat on you, because I love you, and I'm committed to loving you through thick and thin no matter what." That is a better response. It is one born out of Love, not Law. </div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div dir="ltr"><a href="http://bible.com/97/gal.3.5-6.msg" target="_blank">Galatians 3:5-6 Message Version </a></div><div dir="ltr">Answer this question: Does the God who lavishly provides you with his  own presence, his Holy Spirit, working things in your lives you could  never do for yourselves, does he do these things because of your  strenuous moral striving or because you trust him to do them in you?  Don't these things happen among you just as they happened with Abraham?  He believed God, and that act of belief was turned into a life that was  right with God.</div></blockquote><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr">Depending on my own ability is what Law is all about. Whereas, LOVE is about surrendering to Him. The more surrendered I am to him, the more I will be like Him. And my behaviors will change to be like him, without my effort.&nbsp; </div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div dir="ltr"><a href="http://bible.com/97/gal.3.2-4.msg" target="_blank">Galatians 3: 2-4 Message Version</a></div>Let me put this question to you: How did your new life begin? Was it by  working your heads off to please God? Or was it by responding to God's  Message to you? Are you going to continue this craziness? For only crazy  people would think they could complete by their own efforts what was  begun by God. If you weren't smart enough or strong enough to begin it,  how do you suppose you could perfect it? Did you go through this whole  painful learning process for nothing? It is not yet a total loss, but it  certainly will be if you keep this up!</blockquote><div dir="ltr"><br />How do we receive?</div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div dir="ltr"><a href="http://bible.com/59/gal.3.2.ESV" target="_blank">Galatians 3:2 ESV Version </a></div>Let me ask you only this: Did you receive the Spirit by works of the law or by hearing with faith?</blockquote><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr">Knowledge vs Life | Law vs Grace</div><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><div dir="ltr"><a href="http://bible.com/59/rom.7.6.ESV" target="_blank">Romans 7:6 ESV Version </a></div>But now we are released from the law, having died to that which held us  captive, so that we serve in the new way of the Spirit and not in the  old way of the written code.</blockquote><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr">Married to Law or Grace, choose one it cannot be both. </div><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><a href="http://bible.com/59/rom.7.4.ESV" target="_blank">Romans 7:4 ESV Version </a><br />Likewise, my brothers, you also have died to the law through the body  of Christ, so that you may belong to another, to him who has been raised  from the dead, in order that we may bear fruit for God.</blockquote><div dir="ltr"><br /></div><div dir="ltr"><br /><br />The key to living successfully in the Kingdom of God: #<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2014/10/be-still-and-be-led-by-his-still-small.html" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a>? <br /><br /><br />---<br /><h4>And now I leave you with a question:</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>What do you think about Grace?</b></blockquote><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;">To Respond Leave A Comment on This Post</span><br /><br /><h4><div><script src="https://apis.google.com/js/plusone.js"></script><g:plus action="share"></g:plus></div></h4><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><div><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;" target="_blank">About This Blog, and Darrell.&nbsp;</a><br /><br /></div><div><h4><br /><span style="font-weight: bold;">This Weeks Resource:</span></h4><div><ul><li><a href="http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/0883687305/ref=as_li_tl?ie=UTF8&amp;camp=1789&amp;creative=390957&amp;creativeASIN=0883687305&amp;linkCode=as2&amp;tag=your0f4d-20&amp;linkId=3BFJVIAX35BOFKTJ">Grace: The Power To Change, Dr Jim Richards</a><img alt="" border="0" src="http://ir-na.amazon-adsystem.com/e/ir?t=your0f4d-20&amp;l=as2&amp;o=1&amp;a=0883687305" height="1" style="border: none !important; margin: 0px !important;" width="1" /> </li></ul><br /><iframe frameborder="0" marginheight="0" marginwidth="0" scrolling="no" src="//ws-na.amazon-adsystem.com/widgets/q?ServiceVersion=20070822&amp;OneJS=1&amp;Operation=GetAdHtml&amp;MarketPlace=US&amp;source=ss&amp;ref=ss_til&amp;ad_type=product_link&amp;tracking_id=your0f4d-20&amp;marketplace=amazon&amp;region=US&amp;placement=0883687305&amp;asins=0883687305&amp;linkId=ELKK4KX533GMNN52&amp;show_border=true&amp;link_opens_in_new_window=true" style="height: 240px; width: 120px;"></iframe><br /><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16px;">Affiliate Disclosure:</span></i></span><br /><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16px;">I earn&nbsp;commissions&nbsp;off of some of my suggestions.&nbsp;</span></i><i style="line-height: 16px;">Full Disclaimers (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i><br /><i>See my Store for more suggestions (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/books-resources.html" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i></span></div><div>- <br /><br /><!-- Begin MailChimp Signup Form --><link href="//cdn-images.mailchimp.com/embedcode/slim-081711.css" rel="stylesheet" type="text/css"></link><style type="text/css"> #mc_embed_signup{background:#fff; clear:left; font:14px Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif;  width:200px;}  /* Add your own MailChimp form style overrides in your site stylesheet or in this style block.     We recommend moving this block and the preceding CSS link to the HEAD of your HTML file. */ </style><br /><div id="mc_embed_signup"><form action="//darrellwolfe.us3.list-manage.com/subscribe/post?u=979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba&amp;id=14df3ba97b" class="validate" id="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" method="post" name="mc-embedded-subscribe-form" novalidate="" target="_blank"><label for="mce-EMAIL">Subscribe By Email</label> <input class="email" id="mce-EMAIL" name="EMAIL" placeholder="email address" required="" type="email" /> <!-- real people should not fill this in and expect good things - do not remove this or risk form bot signups--><br /><div style="left: -5000px; position: absolute;"><input name="b_979cb0db6ca74343e82a059ba_14df3ba97b" tabindex="-1" /></div><div class="clear"><input class="button" id="mc-embedded-subscribe" name="subscribe" type="submit" value="Subscribe" /></div></form></div><!--End mc_embed_signup--><br /><br /><br /></div></div><br /><br /></div>
